title: vicia tenuifolia subsp. variabilis
intro: Vicia tenuifolia subsp. variabilis is a subspecies of the Vicia tenuifolia plant, commonly known as slender vetch or narrow-leaved vetch. It is an annual or biennial legume native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its long, narrow leaves and purple flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and promote digestion, making it a natural remedy for various gastrointestinal issues. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or dried extract, and is often used to aid in the treatment of stomach ulcers, diarrhea, and other digestive problems.
